title_ru: Глагол to be: am / is / are
explanation_ru:
В английском почти всегда нужен глагол. Когда мы говорим «я студент», «она дома», «они устали», в русском глагола часто нет, а в английском нужен be: am / is / are.
am используем только с I. is — с he, she, it и одним предметом/человеком. are — с we, you, they и множественным числом.
formula:
I → am
he/she/it → is
we/you/they → are

examples.en: My keys are on the table.

explanation_ru: Чтобы сделать вопрос с am/is/are, перенеси am/is/are перед подлежащим. В русском мы часто меняем только интонацию, а в английском порядок слов меняется обязательно.
formula:
Утверждение: You are tired.
Вопрос: Are you tired?
Wh-question: Where are you from?

common_mistakes.explanation: В вопросе are ставим перед you.

explanation_ru: Present Continuous нужен, когда действие происходит сейчас, в момент речи: я сейчас работаю, она сейчас ест, они сейчас играют. Строится через am/is/are + глагол с -ing.
formula:
I am + V-ing
he/she/it is + V-ing
we/you/they are + V-ing

explanation_ru: В вопросе Present Continuous am/is/are ставится перед подлежащим: Are you working? Is she sleeping? В специальных вопросах сначала идёт what/where/why, потом am/is/are.
formula:
Am/Is/Are + subject + V-ing?
What/Where/Why + am/is/are + subject + V-ing?

questions.question: Choose the correct question.
questions.explanation_correct: В вопросе are стоит перед you.

title_ru: Present Simple: I do / he works
explanation_ru: Present Simple описывает факты, привычки и регулярные действия: я работаю, она живёт, магазины открываются. С he/she/it к глаголу обычно добавляем -s или -es.
formula:
I/we/you/they + verb
he/she/it + verb-s / verb-es

questions.question: I ___ coffee.
questions.explanation_correct: С I используем обычный глагол без -s.

explanation_wrong.going: Не хватает is, и смысл другой.

questions.question: My brother ___ a shower every day.

explanation_wrong.going: Не хватает are, и смысл другой.

explanation_ru: Для отрицания в Present Simple используем don't или doesn't. После don't/doesn't глагол всегда в обычной форме: doesn't like, doesn't go, doesn't have.
formula:
I/we/you/they don't + verb
he/she/it doesn't + verb

explanation_wrong.am not: am not не строит отрицание с like.

explanation_wrong.isn't: С обычным глаголом drink нужен doesn't.

questions.question: Choose the correct sentence.
questions.explanation_correct: После doesn't глагол в обычной форме.

explanation_ru: В вопросах Present Simple используем do или does. Does — с he/she/it. После does основной глагол идёт без -s: Does she live? а не Does she lives?
formula:
Do + I/we/you/they + verb?
Does + he/she/it + verb?

questions.question: How often ___ you wash your hair?

explanation_ru: Present Continuous — действие происходит сейчас. Present Simple — привычка, факт, расписание. Слова now, at the moment часто ведут к continuous; always, usually, often, every day — к simple.
formula:
Сейчас: am/is/are + V-ing
Обычно/всегда: Present Simple

examples.en: She is wearing a coat today.

common_mistakes.explanation: like обычно не используется в continuous.

explanation_wrong.do: do не нужен в утверждении continuous.

questions.question: I usually ___ to work by car.

explanation_wrong.am going: usually показывает привычку, не действие сейчас.

explanation_wrong.does: do/does не используется в утверждении continuous.

questions.question: What ___ you usually do at weekends?
questions.explanation_correct: usually + do в вопросе Present Simple.

questions.question: I ___ understand.
questions.explanation_correct: Understand — обычный глагол, отрицание через don't.

explanation_wrong.am not: Не говорим I am not understand.

questions.question: Tom ___ a shower every morning.
questions.explanation_correct: every morning = Present Simple; Tom = he → has.

explanation_ru: Have и have got часто значат «иметь»: у меня есть машина, у неё есть брат. В британском английском have got очень частотен в разговорной речи. В отрицании: haven't got / doesn't have.
formula:
I/you/we/they have / have got
he/she/it has / has got
I haven't got = I don't have

explanation_ru: Was/were — прошедшее время от am/is/are. Используем, когда говорим «был/была/были».
formula:
I/he/she/it → was
we/you/they → were
negative: wasn't / weren't

explanation_ru: Past Simple нужен для законченного действия в прошлом: вчера, в прошлом году, два дня назад. У правильных глаголов добавляем -ed, у неправильных форма особая.
formula:
regular: verb + -ed
irregular: go → went, get → got, see → saw

examples.en: She went to London last week.
examples.ru: Она ездила в Лондон на прошлой неделе.

explanation_ru: В отрицаниях и вопросах Past Simple используем did/didn't. После did/didn't основной глагол возвращается в обычную форму: didn't go, Did you see?
formula:
negative: subject + didn't + verb
question: Did + subject + verb?

questions.question: ___ you see Tom?
questions.explanation_correct: В Past Simple вопрос через did.

explanation_ru: Past Continuous показывает действие, которое происходило в определённый момент в прошлом: я был в процессе. Часто переводится «я делал», «я сидел», «она смотрела».
formula:
I/he/she/it was + V-ing
we/you/they were + V-ing

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: He watched TV at that moment.

explanation_ru: Past Continuous — фон/процесс в прошлом. Past Simple — короткое законченное действие. Часто: I was doing something when something happened.
formula:
process: was/were + V-ing
finished action: V2 / -ed
I was cooking when he called.

examples.en: I was walking home when it started to rain.
examples.ru: Я шёл домой, когда начался дождь.

examples.en: She was reading when I came in.

common_mistakes.wrong: I walked when it started to rain.
common_mistakes.correct: I was walking when it started to rain.
When I came, she was reading.

questions.question: When I came home, she ___ dinner.
It ___ to rain while we were walking.
They were sleeping when the phone ___.
I ___ my keys while I was running.
Choose correct.
options: I was driving when I saw him.
I drove when I was seeing him.
I was drive when I saw him.
questions.correct_answer: I was driving when I saw him.

explanation_ru: Present Perfect связывает прошлое с настоящим. Действие уже случилось, и сейчас важен результат: я потерял ключи — сейчас их нет; она ушла — сейчас её нет.
formula:
I/we/you/they have + V3
he/she/it has + V3

common_mistakes.explanation: Если важен результат сейчас — Present Perfect.

explanation_ru: Just = только что. Already = уже. Yet = ещё/уже в вопросах и отрицаниях, обычно стоит в конце. Эти слова часто используются с Present Perfect.
formula:
have/has just + V3
have/has already + V3
haven't/hasn't + V3 + yet
Have/Has + subject + V3 + yet?

common_mistakes.explanation: Yet в отрицании обычно в конце.

explanation_ru: Have you ever ...? спрашивает об опыте в жизни: ты когда-нибудь был, видел, пробовал? В ответе без точного времени часто используем Present Perfect.
formula: Have/Has + subject + ever + V3?

examples.en: Have you ever been to London?

questions.question: Have you ever ___ this book?
questions.explanation_correct: read имеет одинаковое написание V1/V2/V3, здесь V3.

explanation_ru: How long have you ...? спрашивает «как долго» ситуация продолжается до настоящего момента. В русском часто настоящее время: Я живу здесь 2 года. В английском: I have lived here for two years.
formula:
How long have/has + subject + V3?
for + period
since + starting point

examples.en: How long have you lived here?
I have lived here for two years.

common_mistakes.wrong: I live here for two years.
common_mistakes.correct: I have lived here for two years.
common_mistakes.explanation: Если началось в прошлом и продолжается сейчас — Present Perfect.

common_mistakes.wrong: How long do you know him?
common_mistakes.correct: How long have you known him?

questions.question: I have known him ___ five years.
I have had this car ___ two months.
Choose correct.
options: How long have you known her?
How long do you know her?
How long have you know her?
questions.correct_answer: How long have you known her?

questions.question: He has ___ here all his life.

explanation_ru: For — длительность: for two days. Since — точка начала: since Monday. Ago — сколько времени назад: two days ago, используется с Past Simple.
formula:
for + period
since + start
period + ago

examples.en: I have lived here for ten years.

common_mistakes.wrong: I have arrived two days ago.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: I have known her since May.
I have known her for May.
questions.correct_answer: I have known her since May.

explanation_ru: Present Perfect — опыт/результат без точного времени или связь с настоящим. Past Simple — законченное действие с точным прошлым временем: yesterday, last week, in 2020, ago.
formula:
Present Perfect: have/has + V3
Past Simple: V2 / -ed

examples.en: Have you ever been to Rome?

common_mistakes.wrong: I lost my keys. I can't find them.
common_mistakes.correct: I have lost my keys. I can't find them.
common_mistakes.explanation: Сейчас ключей нет — Present Perfect лучше.

questions.question: I ___ my phone. I can't find it.

explanation_ru: Passive нужен, когда важнее действие или объект, а не тот, кто сделал. На русском часто переводится «сделано», «построено», «написано».
formula:
Present passive: am/is/are + V3
Past passive: was/were + V3

examples.en: The room is cleaned every day.

questions.question: The house ___ built in 2001.
questions.explanation_correct: House = it, past passive: was built.

title_ru: Passive: is being done / has been done
explanation_ru: Эти формы показывают пассив в процессе или результат пассива. Is being done = это делают сейчас. Has been done = это уже сделано.
formula:
is/are being + V3
has/have been + V3

common_mistakes.correct: The road is being repaired now.
common_mistakes.explanation: Если процесс сейчас — is being repaired.

common_mistakes.wrong: The window has broken by someone.

questions.question: The windows have ___ cleaned.
questions.explanation_correct: Present Perfect Passive: have been + V3.

title_ru: be / have / do в разных временах
explanation_ru: Be, have и do — служебные и смысловые глаголы. Be помогает continuous/passive. Have помогает perfect. Do помогает вопросам и отрицаниям в Present/Past Simple.
formula:
be: am/is/are, was/were
have: have/has/had + V3
do: do/does/did for questions/negatives

questions.question: She ___ not at home.
questions.explanation_correct: be как смысловой глагол: is not.

explanation_ru: В английском есть правильные глаголы с -ed и неправильные глаголы с особыми формами. Для Past Simple нужна вторая форма, для Present Perfect — третья форма.
formula:
regular: work-worked-worked
irregular: go-went-gone, see-saw-seen

explanation_ru: Used to описывает прошлую привычку или состояние, которого сейчас нет. На русском: раньше я..., бывало я..., раньше он жил...
formula:
subject + used to + verb
negative: didn't use to + verb
question: Did + subject + use to + verb?

examples.en: She used to live in Rome.
I didn't use to drink coffee.

common_mistakes.wrong: I use to play when I was a child.
common_mistakes.correct: I used to play when I was a child.

common_mistakes.wrong: I didn't used to like tea.
common_mistakes.correct: I didn't use to like tea.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: I didn't used to live here.

questions.question: We didn't use to ___ much money.

title_ru: Future arrangements: What are you doing tomorrow?
explanation_ru: Present Continuous может говорить о будущем, если это личная договорённость или план: встречаюсь, еду, лечу, работаю завтра.
formula: am/is/are + V-ing + future time

examples.en: We are going to the cinema tonight.
examples.ru: Мы идём в кино сегодня вечером.

common_mistakes.explanation: Для личной договорённости лучше Present Continuous.

questions.question: We ___ going to a party on Saturday.
Choose correct.
options: I am seeing the doctor tomorrow.
questions.correct_answer: I am seeing the doctor tomorrow.

explanation_ru: Be going to используем для планов/намерений и для очевидного будущего по признакам сейчас: я собираюсь, оно сейчас случится.
formula: am/is/are going to + verb

examples.en: I am going to buy a car.
Look at the clouds. It is going to rain.
examples.ru: Посмотри на облака. Сейчас будет дождь.

examples.en: We are going to study English.

questions.question: We are going to ___ a new flat.
Choose correct.
options: She is going to buy it.
questions.correct_answer: She is going to buy it.

explanation_ru: Will часто используется для решения в момент речи, обещания, предложения помощи и общей будущей информации. Отрицание: won't.
formula:
will + verb
won't + verb
Shall I/we...? для предложения

explanation_ru: Will также используется для прогнозов, мнений о будущем и фактов, которые, как мы думаем, случатся. Часто вместе с I think, probably, maybe.
formula:
I think + subject + will + verb
probably will / probably won't

examples.en: I don't think he will come.

common_mistakes.correct: I think it will rain tomorrow.

common_mistakes.wrong: I think he won't not come.
common_mistakes.correct: I don't think he will come.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: I think it will be cold.
I think it is be cold.
questions.correct_answer: I think it will be cold.

explanation_ru: Might означает «возможно». Используем, когда мы не уверены: может пойти дождь, может он придёт. После might глагол всегда в обычной форме.
formula:
might + verb
might not + verb

explanation_ru: Can означает «мочь/уметь»: I can swim. Could — прошедшая форма can или более вежливая просьба: Could you help me? После can/could глагол всегда без to.
formula:
can + verb
can't + verb
could + verb
Could you...? = вежливая просьба

examples.ru: Не могли бы вы открыть окно?

questions.question: She can't ___ a car.
questions.explanation_correct: После can't глагол без to и без -s.

explanation_wrong.Do: Можно Do you help?, но это другой смысл.

questions.question: He ___ play the guitar when he was young.
questions.explanation_correct: when he was young → прошлая способность.

questions.question: Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: Can не меняется и не требует to.

explanation_ru: Must выражает необходимость: нужно, обязан. Mustn't — строгий запрет: нельзя. Needn't — нет необходимости: не нужно, можно не делать.
formula:
must + verb
mustn't + verb = нельзя
needn't + verb = не нужно

questions.question: We ___ hurry. We have a lot of time.

explanation_wrong.mustn't: mustn't = запрещено, это не тот смысл.

questions.question: Children ___ play with matches.
questions.explanation_correct: Это запрет: детям нельзя играть со спичками.

explanation_ru: Should означает «следует / стоит». Используем для совета или мнения о правильном действии. После should глагол без to.
formula:
should + verb
shouldn't + verb

examples.ru: Тебе не стоит так много работать.

questions.question: What ___ I do?
questions.explanation_correct: What should I do? — просьба о совете.

explanation_wrong.do: What do I do? другой смысл.
do not be late возможен как императив, но не здесь.

explanation_ru: Have to означает «приходится / нужно», часто потому что есть правило, работа, обстоятельства. В вопросах и отрицаниях используем do/does/did: Do you have to...? I don't have to...
formula:
I/you/we/they have to + verb
he/she/it has to + verb
negative: don't/doesn't have to
question: Do/Does ... have to?

examples.en: She has to get up early.

questions.question: I ___ have to wear a uniform.

explanation_ru: Would like — вежливая форма «хотел бы». Используем в предложениях, просьбах и желаниях. После would like часто идёт существительное или to + verb.
formula:
would like + noun
would like to + verb
Would you like...?

common_mistakes.explanation: Для желания сейчас вежливее would like.
После would like перед глаголом нужен to.

explanation_wrong.for: for не подходит.

questions.question: She'd like ___ new phone.
questions.explanation_correct: new phone — существительное, to не нужно.

explanation_wrong.an: new начинается с согласного звука.

questions.question: Would you like to ___ with us?

explanation_wrong.do: Do like = любить обычно, не хотел бы.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: Would you like to a drink?
Do you would like a drink?

explanation_ru: There is / there are означает «есть / находится / имеется». There is — для одного предмета или неисчисляемого, there are — для множественного числа.
formula:
there is + singular/uncountable
there are + plural

examples.en: There is a book on the table.

common_mistakes.wrong: It is a book on the table.
common_mistakes.correct: There is a book on the table.

questions.question: There ___ a cat in the room.

explanation_wrong.Do: С there is вопрос через is.
С there are вопрос через are.

questions.question: There ___ no time.
questions.explanation_correct: Time неисчисляемое, there is no time.

title_ru: There was/were, there has/have been, there will be
explanation_ru: Эта тема расширяет there is/are в прошлое, perfect и будущее: there was/were, there has/have been, there will be.
formula:
past: there was/were
perfect: there has/have been
future: there will be

examples.en: There will be a meeting tomorrow.

questions.question: There ___ a lot of people at the party.
There ___ a mistake in your answer.
___ there any problems yesterday?
questions.explanation_correct: Problems plural + past question = were there.

questions.question: There ___ no cars in the street.

explanation_ru: It используется как формальное подлежащее, когда говорим о погоде, времени, расстоянии или ситуации в целом. В русском подлежащего часто нет: холодно, поздно, далеко.
formula:
It is + adjective
It is + time/weather/distance

common_mistakes.explanation: Можно Today is cold, но базовая форма — It is cold today.

explanation_ru: В коротких ответах и коротких фразах часто используем вспомогательный глагол вместо повторения всей фразы: Yes, I am. No, I don't. She can, but I can't.
formula: use the auxiliary: am/is/are, do/does/did, have/has, can/will

examples.en: Are you tired? Yes, I am.
Do you like coffee? No, I don't.
Can she swim? Yes, she can.

common_mistakes.wrong: Does he work? Yes, he works.

questions.question: Are you hungry? Yes, I ___.
questions.explanation_correct: Вопрос с are → ответ с am.

questions.question: Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: Для вопроса Are you ready? короткий ответ: Yes, I am.

title_ru: Have you? Are you? Don't you?
explanation_ru: Короткие вопросы типа Have you? Are you? Don't you? используют auxiliary из предыдущей фразы. Они помогают переспросить, удивиться или продолжить разговор.
formula:
Statement auxiliary → short question
I have... → Have you?
I don't... → Don't you?

examples.note: Повторяем am как are для you.

examples.en: I don't like tea. — Don't you?

common_mistakes.explanation: am требует are в ответе к you.

questions.question: He has gone. — ___ he?
questions.explanation_correct: Present Perfect has gone → Has he?

questions.question: Choose correct reply: I like coffee.

title_ru: too / either / so am I / neither do I
explanation_ru: Too/either и so/neither помогают сказать «я тоже» или «я тоже не». Too — в положительных фразах, either — в отрицательных. So/Neither требуют правильный auxiliary.
formula:
positive: I am too / So am I
negative: I am not either / Neither am I

examples.en: I am tired. — I am too.
I don't like tea. — I don't either.
examples.ru: Я не люблю чай. — Я тоже не.

examples.en: I can swim. — So can I.

common_mistakes.wrong: I don't like tea. — I don't too.
common_mistakes.correct: I don't like tea. — I don't either.

common_mistakes.wrong: I am tired. — So do I.
common_mistakes.correct: I am tired. — So am I.

common_mistakes.wrong: She can't drive. — Neither do I.
common_mistakes.correct: She can't drive. — Neither can I.

explanation_wrong.neither: neither используется с auxiliary: Neither do I.

questions.question: She has been there. — So ___ I.
Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: Для I am tired ответ: I am too.

questions.question: I went home. — So ___ I.
questions.explanation_correct: Past Simple action → So did I.

explanation_wrong.went: В этой конструкции нужен auxiliary did.

explanation_ru: Отрицание зависит от типа глагола. С be: am not/isn't/aren't. С обычными глаголами: don't/doesn't/didn't. С perfect: haven't/hasn't.
formula:
be → am not/isn't/aren't
Present Simple → don't/doesn't
Past Simple → didn't
Perfect → haven't/hasn't

questions.question: Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: С I + be отрицание: I'm not.

В стандартном английском говорят I'm not.

questions.question: We ___ have a car.
questions.explanation_correct: have как обычный глагол: don't have.

explanation_wrong.haven't: Вариант possible в BrE с got: haven't got, но тут без got — don't have.

explanation_ru: В английском вопрос строится через вспомогательный глагол перед подлежащим: Are you...? Do you...? Have you...? Нельзя оставлять порядок как в утверждении.
formula:
auxiliary + subject + main verb?
Where/What/Why + auxiliary + subject + verb?

questions.question: Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: Present Simple question: do + subject + verb.

title_ru: Who saw you? / Who did you see?
explanation_ru: Если who/what — подлежащее, do/does/did не нужен: Who saw you? Если who/what — объект, нужен auxiliary: Who did you see?
formula:
subject question: Who saw you?
object question: Who did you see?

common_mistakes.explanation: Если who — подлежащее, did не нужен.

explanation_wrong.Who did: did не нужен, если who subject.

explanation_wrong.did: Если what subject, did не нужен.

explanation_wrong.did write: В обычном subject question did не нужен.

explanation_wrong.Who did call you?: В нейтральном вопросе did не нужен.

questions.question: Who ___ you talking to?
questions.explanation_correct: Present Continuous: Who are you talking to?

title_ru: Who is she talking to? / What is it like?
explanation_ru: В вопросах с предлогами предлог часто стоит в конце: Who are you talking to? What are you looking at? What is it like? — Какой он/это?
formula: question word + auxiliary + subject + verb + preposition?

common_mistakes.explanation: В разговорном английском предлог часто в конце.

explanation_wrong.at: look at, не wait at.

explanation_wrong.for: look for = искать, другой смысл.

explanation_wrong.at: talk at возможно, но другой оттенок.
explanation_wrong.for: Не подходит.

explanation_wrong.look: What does it look like? другая структура.

questions.question: Where are you ___ from?
questions.explanation_correct: Where are you from? без предлога в конце.

explanation_wrong.at: Неверно.

explanation_wrong.for: Неверно.

questions.question: Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: Предлог with нужен и обычно стоит в конце.

explanation_wrong.With who did you go?: Формально возможно, но менее разговорно; whom было бы формальнее.

explanation_wrong.from: Неверно.

explanation_ru: What спрашивает «что/какой», когда выбор широкий. Which — «который», когда выбор ограничен. How — «как/насколько/сколько» в выражениях типа how old, how much.
formula:
What + noun?
Which + limited choice?
How + adjective/adverb?

explanation_wrong.Which: Можно при выборе, но базово what colour.

questions.question: ___ one do you want, tea or coffee?

explanation_wrong.What: What возможно шире, но при выборе лучше which.

explanation_ru: How long does it take? спрашивает, сколько времени занимает действие. Ответ часто: It takes ten minutes. С человеком: It takes me ten minutes to get there.
formula:
How long does it take?
It takes + time
It takes + person + time + to + verb

examples.en: It takes me an hour to get to work.
examples.ru: Мне нужен час, чтобы добраться до работы.

common_mistakes.explanation: Стандартный вопрос: How long does it take?

common_mistakes.wrong: It takes me an hour get there.
common_mistakes.correct: It takes me an hour to get there.

questions.question: It takes me an hour ___ get there.

explanation_wrong.for: Не перед глаголом.

questions.question: It ___ us two hours to drive there.

explanation_wrong.for: Не перед verb.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: How much long does it take?

title_ru: Do you know where...? / I don't know what...
explanation_ru: В косвенных вопросах после Do you know... / I don't know... порядок слов как в утверждении: where he lives, what it means. Не используем do/does/did внутри второй части.
formula:
Do you know + question word + subject + verb?
I don't know + question word + subject + verb

examples.en: Do you know where she lives?
I don't know what it means.
examples.ru: Я не знаю, что это значит.
examples.note: it means, не does it mean.

examples.en: Can you tell me where the station is?

common_mistakes.wrong: Do you know where does she live?
common_mistakes.correct: Do you know where she lives?

common_mistakes.wrong: I don't know what does it mean.
common_mistakes.correct: I don't know what it means.

common_mistakes.wrong: Can you tell me where is the station?
common_mistakes.correct: Can you tell me where the station is?

questions.question: Can you tell me where the bank ___?
Do you know what time ___?
questions.explanation_correct: Косвенный вопрос: what time it is.

questions.question: I don't know where they ___.
questions.explanation_correct: Косвенный вопрос в Past Simple без did.

questions.question: Could you tell me how much it ___?
Choose correct.
options: Do you know where he works?
Do you know where does he work?
Do you know where works he?
questions.correct_answer: Do you know where he works?

questions.question: I don't know why she ___ angry.

title_ru: She said that / He told me that
explanation_ru: Reported speech передаёт чужие слова. Say обычно без объекта: She said that... Tell требует объект: He told me that... В базовой грамматике важно различать said и told.
formula:
say/said + that
 tell/told + person + that

examples.en: She said that she was tired.
He told me that he was busy.

common_mistakes.correct: He told me that... / He said that...

common_mistakes.wrong: She told that she was tired.
common_mistakes.correct: She said that she was tired.

questions.question: He ___ me that he was busy.

explanation_wrong.told me: Тогда было бы told me they...

questions.question: She told ___ that she was leaving.

explanation_wrong.to me: He said to me возможно, но дальше нужно that; базовая форма лучше He told me.

explanation_wrong.spoke: spoke to us, но не spoke us.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: He told me he was tired.
He said me he was tired.
He told to me he was tired.
questions.correct_answer: He told me he was tired.

explanation_wrong.told me: told me goodbye возможно в AmE, но базово say goodbye.

title_ru: work / working, go / going, do / doing
explanation_ru: После некоторых конструкций нужна форма -ing, после других — обычный глагол. Continuous требует V-ing; после can/must/should/will — обычная форма.
formula:
be + V-ing
modal + V1
like/enjoy + V-ing

explanation_wrong.to read: В базовой форме с enjoy — reading.

questions.question: She likes ___.
questions.explanation_correct: like + -ing часто используется для общего предпочтения.

explanation_wrong.dance: Возможен like to dance, но без to лучше dancing.

explanation_ru: После одних глаголов используется to + verb, после других -ing. Want/decide/hope часто требуют to. Enjoy/finish/mind требуют -ing.
formula:
want/decide/hope + to + verb
enjoy/finish/mind + V-ing

explanation_wrong.to talk: stop to talk = остановиться, чтобы поговорить, другой смысл.

title_ru: I want you to / I told you to
explanation_ru: Конструкция object + to + verb: I want you to help. I told him to wait. В русском это часто «хочу, чтобы ты...» или «сказал ему...».
formula: want/tell/ask/would like + object + to + verb

examples.en: I want you to help me.
examples.ru: Я хочу, чтобы ты помог мне.

common_mistakes.wrong: I want that you help me.
common_mistakes.correct: I want you to help me.

common_mistakes.wrong: I asked to him to come.

explanation_wrong.to not go: В базовом курсе лучше not to go.

title_ru: I went to the shop to...
explanation_ru: To + verb может показывать цель: зачем я что-то сделал. I went to the shop to buy bread = пошёл в магазин, чтобы купить хлеб.
formula: action + to + verb = purpose

examples.en: I went to the shop to buy bread.
examples.ru: Я пошёл в магазин, чтобы купить хлеб.

examples.en: She called me to ask a question.

common_mistakes.wrong: I went to the shop for to buy.
common_mistakes.correct: I went to the shop to buy.

questions.question: I went to the shop ___ milk.

explanation_wrong.for buy: После for нужен noun/ing, не V1.
explanation_wrong.buying: Не показывает цель в этой структуре.

explanation_wrong.for help: for help = за помощью, другой смысл.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: I came here to see you.
I came here for see you.
I came here for to see you.
questions.correct_answer: I came here to see you.

topic: go to go on go for go ing
title_ru: go to / go on / go for / go -ing
explanation_ru: После go используются разные модели: go to a place, go on holiday/a trip, go for a walk/swim, go shopping/swimming. Это устойчивые конструкции.
formula:
go to + place
go on + trip/holiday
go for + walk/swim
go + V-ing

examples.en: I go to work by bus.
examples.ru: Я езжу на работу на автобусе.

explanation_wrong.for: Не здесь.
explanation_wrong.on: Не здесь.
Не базовая форма.

explanation_wrong.for: Не базовая форма.
Неверно.
explanation_wrong.on: Неверно.

explanation_wrong.for: Не базовая конструкция.

explanation_ru: Get — очень частотный глагол с разными значениями: получить, стать, добраться, купить. Значение зависит от контекста.
formula:
get + noun = receive/buy
get + adjective = become
get to + place = arrive

examples.en: How do I get to the station?

common_mistakes.correct: How can I get to the station?

questions.question: How do I get ___ the airport?

explanation_wrong.at: arrive at, но get to.
explanation_wrong.in: Не подходит.

questions.question: What time did you get ___ home?

explanation_wrong.at: Не здесь.
Неверно.

explanation_ru: Do часто связан с действиями, работой, заданиями. Make — с созданием/производством результата. Многие выражения нужно запомнить как устойчивые.
formula:
do homework/work/exercises
make coffee/a mistake/a decision/a noise

Можно создавать упражнения, но не делать зарядку.

explanation_ru: Have может значить «иметь», но также входит в устойчивые выражения: have breakfast, have a shower, have a good time. В отрицаниях/вопросах часто используем do/does/did.
formula:
have + noun
have breakfast/lunch/dinner
have a shower/bath
have a good time

examples.en: She has a shower every morning.
Did you have a good time?

explanation_wrong.an: headache начинается с consonant sound.

explanation_wrong.make: make dinner = готовить ужин, но вопрос о еде обычно have.

explanation_ru: Местоимения меняются по роли в предложении. I/he/she/we/they — кто делает действие. Me/him/her/us/them — объект действия: меня, его, её, нас, их.
formula:
subject: I, he, she, we, they
object: me, him, her, us, them

Her не subject, I не object.

questions.question: Give it to ___.
questions.explanation_correct: После to нужна object form her.

explanation_ru: My, your, his, her, its, our, their показывают принадлежность и стоят перед существительным: my car, her phone, their house.
formula: my/your/his/her/its/our/their + noun

explanation_ru: Mine, yours, his, hers, ours, theirs используются без существительного: This is my book → This is mine. Whose...? спрашивает «чей?»
formula:
my + noun → mine
your + noun → yours
her + noun → hers
our + noun → ours
their + noun → theirs

examples.en: This is my bag. It's mine.

common_mistakes.correct: This is my bag. / This bag is mine.

common_mistakes.explanation: Если есть noun phone, он стоит после whose.

title_ru: I / me / my / mine: формы местоимений
explanation_ru: В английском одно и то же лицо выражается разными формами: I — я как подлежащее, me — меня/мне как объект, my — мой перед существительным, mine — мой без существительного.
formula:
I = subject
me = object
my + noun
mine = without noun

examples.en: This is my book. It's mine.
examples.note: my перед book, mine без book.

questions.question: She gave it to ___.
questions.explanation_correct: После предлога to нужна object form.

Me не может быть подлежащим, she не object.

explanation_ru: Возвратные местоимения показывают, что действие направлено на самого человека: I cut myself — я порезался. Также используются для акцента: I did it myself — я сделал это сам.
formula:
I → myself
you → yourself/yourselves
he → himself
she → herself
we → ourselves
they → themselves

examples.en: She looked at herself in the mirror.
examples.ru: Она посмотрела на себя в зеркало.

common_mistakes.explanation: Когда объект = тот же человек, нужен myself.

common_mistakes.wrong: She looked at her in the mirror.
common_mistakes.correct: She looked at herself in the mirror.
common_mistakes.explanation: Если она смотрела на себя — herself.

questions.question: He looked at ___ in the mirror.

explanation_wrong.you: В этой конструкции нужна возвратная форма.

explanation_ru: 's показывает принадлежность: Ann's camera = камера Анны. Для множественного числа на -s обычно добавляем только апостроф: my parents' house.
formula:
person + 's + noun
plural ending -s + ' + noun

explanation_ru: A/an ставится перед одним исчисляемым существительным, когда мы говорим о нём не как о конкретном известном предмете. A перед согласным звуком, an перед гласным звуком.
formula:
a + consonant sound
an + vowel sound
singular countable noun usually needs an article

common_mistakes.explanation: Перед singular countable noun нужен артикль.

explanation_wrong.an: an перед vowel sound.
doctor не vowel sound.
Смотрим на звук, не букву.

questions.question: It's ___ hour.
questions.explanation_correct: Hour начинается с гласного звука, h не произносится.

explanation_ru: Большинство существительных получают -s во множественном числе: flower → flowers. После -s/-sh/-ch/-x добавляем -es. Некоторые формы неправильные: man → men, child → children.
formula:
singular: one book
plural: two books
-s / -es / irregular plural

explanation_ru: Countable nouns можно посчитать: a car, two cars. Uncountable nouns обычно не имеют plural и не используют a/an: water, money, music, rice.
formula:
countable: a/an, plural -s
uncountable: no a/an, usually no plural

common_mistakes.correct: some water / a glass of water
much money / a lot of money

explanation_wrong.an: Неверно.

explanation_wrong.some: some cars/water, но one car → a car.
some apples, но one apple → an apple.

explanation_wrong.many: many с countable plural.

explanation_ru: Для неисчисляемых существительных используем меры и контейнеры: a piece of advice, a bottle of water, a cup of coffee. Так можно сказать «одна штука/порция».
formula:
a piece of + uncountable
a bottle/glass/cup of + liquid
a kilo of / a slice of

common_mistakes.correct: two bottles of water / two glasses of water
a loaf of bread / some bread

explanation_wrong.an: Нельзя an advice.

questions.question: Can you give me a ___ of advice?
Would you like a ___ of coffee?

explanation_wrong.piece: piece of bread возможно, но loaf = буханка.

explanation_ru: A/an используем, когда говорим о чём-то впервые или не конкретном. The используем, когда понятно, о каком именно предмете речь, или он уже упоминался.
formula:
first mention: a/an
known/specific: the

examples.en: I saw a dog. The dog was black.
examples.ru: Я увидел собаку. Эта собака была чёрной.

common_mistakes.wrong: I saw the dog. The dog was black.
common_mistakes.correct: I saw a dog. The dog was black.
common_mistakes.explanation: Если впервые и не конкретная — a.
Если дверь понятна в ситуации — the.

questions.question: I saw ___ cat. ___ cat was white.

explanation_wrong.a: Если любое окно, возможно, но в комнате обычно specific.
explanation_wrong.an: window начинается с consonant sound.

questions.question: I need ___ umbrella.
questions.explanation_correct: Нужен любой зонтик; umbrella vowel sound.

explanation_wrong.the: Если не конкретный, лучше an.

explanation_wrong.an: Неверно.

explanation_wrong.the: Только если конкретная книга известна.
explanation_wrong.an: book consonant sound.

explanation_wrong.A: Речь об известной книге.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: I have a car. The car is red.
I have the car. A car is red.
I have car. The car is red.
questions.correct_answer: I have a car. The car is red.
questions.explanation_correct: First mention a, second mention the.

explanation_wrong.an: light consonant sound.

explanation_ru: The используем, когда предмет уникален, уже известен или конкретизирован: the sun, the door, the book on the table. The не зависит от единственного/множественного числа.
formula:
the + specific noun
the + unique thing
the + noun + of/that/where specific

examples.en: The book on the table is mine.
I like the music in this film.
examples.ru: Мне нравится музыка в этом фильме.

common_mistakes.wrong: Book on the table is mine.
common_mistakes.correct: The book on the table is mine.

explanation_wrong.A: Солнце одно для нас.

explanation_wrong.the: The music = конкретная музыка.

questions.question: I like ___ music in this film.

explanation_wrong.A: Может быть, но обычно known door.

explanation_wrong.for: Неверно.
explanation_wrong.from: Неверно.

title_ru: go to work / go home / go to the cinema
explanation_ru: С некоторыми местами употребление артиклей устойчивое: go to work/school, go home без to, но go to the cinema, go to the station.
formula:
go to work/school
 go home
 go to the cinema/the station

examples.en: I go to work by bus.
examples.ru: Я езжу на работу на автобусе.

common_mistakes.explanation: Work как место работы без the.

explanation_wrong.at the: Возможно для конкретного здания, но базовая фраза at school.

explanation_wrong.to the: Не go to the bed в смысле спать.

explanation_wrong.in the: Может быть конкретное здание, но учебная базовая фраза in hospital.

Только в другом значении nursing home.

title_ru: I like music / I hate exams
explanation_ru: Когда говорим о вещах вообще, часто не используем the: I like music, Dogs are friendly, I hate exams. The нужен, если речь о конкретных вещах.
formula:
general plural/uncountable: no the
specific: the

examples.en: The dogs in this house are noisy.

explanation_wrong.the: The coffee = конкретный кофе.

explanation_wrong.A: A cat singular general possible, but plural general = cats.

explanation_wrong.the: The exams = конкретные экзамены.
explanation_wrong.an: exams plural.

questions.question: ___ coffee in this café is good.

explanation_wrong.A: coffee uncountable.

explanation_wrong.the: The animals = конкретные животные.
explanation_wrong.an: animals plural.

questions.question: ___ animals in this zoo look healthy.

explanation_wrong.A: Plural.

questions.question: Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: Food in general/type of food без the.

explanation_wrong.the: Specific sleep, не здесь.

explanation_ru: С названиями мест артикль зависит от типа названия. Обычно страны и города без the: France, London. Но группы островов, реки, моря, океаны и некоторые страны — с the.
formula:
no the: most countries/cities
 the: rivers/seas/oceans, plural names, the UK, the USA

explanation_wrong.the: Не the Germany.

explanation_wrong.A: Неверно.

explanation_wrong.the: Не the France.

explanation_wrong.A: Не river name.
Plural name.

explanation_ru: This/these — близко. That/those — далеко. This/that для единственного числа, these/those для множественного.
formula:
near singular: this
far singular: that
near plural: these
far plural: those

questions.question: ___ man over there is my teacher.
___ is my friend Anna.
questions.explanation_correct: Introducing one person near you: This is...

explanation_ru: One/ones заменяют уже названное существительное, чтобы не повторять его. One — для единственного числа, ones — для множественного.
formula:
singular: one
plural: ones

examples.en: I need a pen. Do you have one?
examples.ru: Мне нужна ручка. У тебя есть одна?

examples.en: Which shoes do you like? The black ones.

common_mistakes.wrong: Do you have pen? I need one pen.
common_mistakes.correct: Do you have a pen? I need one.
I like this one / these ones.

questions.question: I don't have a phone. I need ___.
Which apples do you want? The red ___.

explanation_wrong.it: Could be, but after this use one.

explanation_wrong.ones: Если plural, но здесь one item.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: I like the small one shoes.

questions.question: Do you want this ___ or that ___?

explanation_wrong.it / it: Не после this/that в такой структуре.

explanation_ru: Some обычно используется в утверждениях, any — в вопросах и отрицаниях. Оба могут использоваться с plural nouns и uncountable nouns.
formula:
positive: some
negative/question: any

explanation_wrong.some: Возможно в предложении, но обычный вопрос = any.

questions.question: There is ___ milk in the fridge.

Any обычно не в положительном утверждении.

explanation_ru: Not any и no часто означают одно и то же: I don't have any money = I have no money. None используется без существительного: None of them.
formula:
not any + noun
no + noun
none = not one / not any

common_mistakes.correct: I don't have any money / I have no money.
I have no money / I don't have any money.
common_mistakes.explanation: Нужен no или don't have any.

explanation_wrong.no: I have no time, но не don't have no.

explanation_wrong.any: В положительной форме any не подходит здесь.

questions.question: I saw ___ people.
questions.explanation_correct: Saw no people = не видел людей.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: I don't know any of them.
I don't know no of them.
questions.correct_answer: I don't know any of them.

explanation_ru: Anybody/anyone/anything обычно используются в вопросах и отрицаниях. Nobody/no-one/nothing уже содержат отрицание, поэтому don't/doesn't с ними обычно не нужен.
formula:
not + anybody/anything
nobody/no-one/nothing + positive verb

examples.en: There is nothing in the bag.

common_mistakes.correct: I don't know anybody / I know nobody.
I didn't see anything / I saw nothing.

explanation_wrong.Anybody: Anybody in positive = кто угодно, не здесь.
Anybody wants = любой хочет, другой смысл.

questions.question: I saw ___.
questions.explanation_correct: I saw nothing = ничего не видел.

explanation_wrong.anything: В positive обычно не этот смысл.

explanation_ru: Some-, any-, no-, every- образуют слова: somebody, anything, nowhere, everywhere. Some- обычно в утверждениях, any- в вопросах/отрицаниях, no- уже отрицание.
formula:
somebody/something/somewhere
anybody/anything/anywhere
nobody/nothing/nowhere
everybody/everything/everywhere

common_mistakes.correct: I didn't go anywhere / I went nowhere.

common_mistakes.explanation: Для «никто не пришёл» лучше nobody came.

explanation_wrong.something: Возможен в предложении, но обычный вопрос = anything.

explanation_wrong.Anything: Anything is ready = что угодно готово, странно.

explanation_ru: Every говорит о каждом отдельном элементе группы и используется с singular noun. All говорит о всей группе вместе и используется с plural/uncountable.
formula:
every + singular noun
all + plural noun / uncountable noun

explanation_wrong.all: All morning = всё утро, другой смысл.

explanation_wrong.Every: Every one of my friends, не every my friends.

topic: all most some any no none
title_ru: All / most / some / any / no / none
explanation_ru: Эти слова показывают количество в группе: all = все, most = большинство, some = некоторые, any = какие-нибудь/никакие в вопросах/отрицаниях, no = никакие, none = ни один.
formula:
all/most/some/any/no + noun
none of + pronoun/determiner

common_mistakes.correct: Most people / Most of the people
No students came / None of the students came.
common_mistakes.explanation: None обычно без noun или none of.

common_mistakes.correct: All students / All of the students

questions.question: ___ my friends live here.
questions.explanation_correct: Перед my используем some of my.

explanation_wrong.Any: Не в positive с этим смыслом.

questions.question: Do you know ___ of these people?

explanation_wrong.no: Do you know no... не базово.

explanation_wrong.Most of: Most of the money, но не all.

Нужно no people или none of.

explanation_ru: Both = оба. Either = любой из двух / один или другой. Neither = ни один из двух. Эти слова используются, когда речь именно о двух людях/предметах.
formula:
both + plural
either + singular
neither + singular
both of / either of / neither of

examples.ru: Можешь сесть на любой из двух автобусов.

questions.question: ___ answer is right.
questions.explanation_correct: Neither + singular, ни один из двух.

explanation_wrong.are: Иногда встречается, но в базовой грамматике is.

questions.question: Either of these roads ___ to town.

explanation_ru: Many используется с исчисляемыми plural nouns, much — с неисчисляемыми nouns, a lot of — с обоими типами и звучит естественно в утверждениях.
formula:
many + plural countable
much + uncountable
a lot of + both

examples.en: She has a lot of friends.

explanation_wrong.many: Many для plural countable.

explanation_wrong.for: Неверно.

explanation_wrong.many: Many для plural countable.

questions.question: We spent ___ money.
questions.explanation_correct: A lot of works with uncountable money.

explanation_wrong.many: Money uncountable.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: I have a lot of work.
questions.correct_answer: I have a lot of work.

explanation_wrong.a lot: Нужен a lot of, но question обычно how many.

explanation_ru: A little используется с неисчисляемыми существительными: a little water. A few — с исчисляемыми plural nouns: a few friends. Little/few без a часто звучат как «мало, недостаточно».
formula:
a little + uncountable
a few + plural countable
little/few = not much/not many

questions.question: There are ___ chairs in the room.

explanation_ru: Прилагательные описывают существительные и обычно стоят перед noun или после be. В английском прилагательное не меняется по числу: a big house, big houses.
formula:
adjective + noun
be + adjective
adjective does not take plural -s

common_mistakes.correct: She is beautiful / She is a beautiful woman.
common_mistakes.explanation: Adjective alone after be, or adjective + noun.

explanation_wrong.well: Adverb/health adjective; for food use good.

questions.question: Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: Adjective before noun and no plural.

explanation_ru: Наречия описывают действие: как человек что-то делает. Часто образуются от adjective + -ly: quick → quickly. Но есть исключения: good → well, fast → fast.
formula:
adjective describes noun
adverb describes verb
quick → quickly
good → well

common_mistakes.explanation: After be describe subject with adjective.

explanation_wrong.quick: Adjective; adverb possible informally, but base grammar uses quickly.

explanation_ru: Comparative сравнивает два предмета/человека: older, bigger, more expensive. К коротким прилагательным часто добавляем -er, к длинным — more.
formula:
short adjective + -er
long adjective: more + adjective

examples.en: My brother is older than me.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: This book is most interesting than.

explanation_ru: После comparative часто используем than: older than, cheaper than, more interesting than. Than вводит второй объект сравнения.
formula: comparative + than

examples.en: This book is more interesting than that one.
My car is faster than yours.

common_mistakes.wrong: She is more tall than me.

questions.question: This phone is ___ expensive than mine.

explanation_wrong.much: Needs more after it: much more expensive.

questions.question: This test is ___ than the last one.
Choose correct.
options: This is more cheap than that.

questions.question: Her job is more difficult ___ mine.

explanation_ru: Not as ... as означает «не такой ... как». Используем обычную форму adjective/adverb между as и as: not as old as, not as expensive as.
formula: not as + adjective/adverb + as

examples.en: This car isn't as expensive as that one.
examples.ru: Эта машина не такая дорогая, как та.

examples.en: I don't run as fast as you.
examples.ru: Я бегаю не так быстро, как ты.

examples.en: She isn't as old as me.
examples.ru: Она не такая взрослая/старая, как я.

common_mistakes.explanation: Внутри as...as обычная форма, не comparative.

questions.question: This room isn't as big ___ that one.
She isn't as ___ as her sister.
questions.explanation_correct: Use base adjective between as and as.

questions.question: I don't work as hard ___ him.

explanation_wrong.than: Use harder than, not as hard than.

questions.question: This test isn't as ___ as I expected.
My car isn't as fast ___ yours.
Choose correct.
options: It isn't as expensive as I thought.
It isn't as more expensive as I thought.
It isn't expensive than I thought.
questions.correct_answer: It isn't as expensive as I thought.

questions.question: He doesn't speak as clearly ___ you.

title_ru: Superlative: the oldest / the most expensive
explanation_ru: Superlative показывает максимум в группе: самый старый, самый дорогой. Обычно используем the: the oldest, the most expensive.
formula:
short adjective: the + adjective-est
long adjective: the most + adjective
irregular: good → the best

questions.question: This is ___ book in the shop.
He is ___ player in the team.
This is ___ day of my life.
Choose correct.
options: This is the most interesting film.
questions.correct_answer: This is the most interesting film.

questions.question: What is ___ river in the world?

explanation_ru: Enough означает «достаточно». С существительными enough стоит перед noun: enough money. С adjectives/adverbs enough стоит после: old enough, quickly enough.
formula:
enough + noun
adjective/adverb + enough
enough to + verb

examples.en: She is old enough to drive.

explanation_wrong.many: Wrong.

questions.question: We have enough money ___ buy it.

explanation_wrong.for: Before noun possible: enough money for it, but before verb to.

questions.question: This bag is big ___ for all my clothes.

explanation_ru: Too означает «слишком» — больше, чем нужно или хорошо. Часто после too используется to + verb: too tired to work.
formula:
too + adjective/adverb
too + adjective/adverb + to + verb

common_mistakes.explanation: С adjective используем too без much.

explanation_wrong.many: Many с plural nouns.

explanation_wrong.for: For перед noun, не verb.

explanation_wrong.too much: Too much + noun/after verb, не перед quickly.
explanation_wrong.many: Не с adverb.

explanation_wrong.for: Перед verb нужен to.
explanation_wrong.at: Неверно.

explanation_wrong.much: Much не с many в этой структуре.

Enough ставится после adjective: expensive enough.

explanation_wrong.enough: Would be old enough, not young enough.

title_ru: Word order 1: He speaks English very well
explanation_ru: В английском порядок слов жёстче, чем в русском. Обычно: subject + verb + object + place/time. Наречие образа действия часто идёт после объекта.
formula:
subject + verb + object
subject + verb + object + adverb
place before time often: at home yesterday

examples.en: I watched TV at home yesterday.

common_mistakes.wrong: I like very much this book.
common_mistakes.correct: I like this book very much.

common_mistakes.wrong: We went yesterday to the cinema.
common_mistakes.correct: We went to the cinema yesterday.

explanation_wrong.with Anna: Met with possible in other sense, but not here.

explanation_ru: Наречия частоты usually, often, always, never обычно стоят перед основным глаголом, но после be. С вспомогательными глаголами — после первого auxiliary.
formula:
before main verb: I usually go
after be: I am usually
between auxiliary and main verb: I have never been

examples.en: I have never been to Italy.
examples.ru: Я никогда не был в Италии.

questions.question: Do you ___ have breakfast?
questions.explanation_correct: After do + subject, before main verb.

questions.question: We are ___ at home in the evening.

explanation_ru: Still = всё ещё. Already = уже. Yet = ещё/уже, чаще в отрицаниях и вопросах и обычно в конце. Важно не путать позиции этих слов.
formula:
still before main verb / after be
already before main verb or after have/has
yet at the end in negatives/questions

common_mistakes.explanation: В базовом порядке yet в конце.

common_mistakes.correct: I have already eaten / Have you eaten yet?
common_mistakes.explanation: Yet не для positive statement like this.

explanation_wrong.yet: Yet usually at end in questions/negatives.
Yet not in positive statement like this.

explanation_wrong.already: Already = уже, не с haven't here.
explanation_wrong.still: Could be still haven't done it, but position differs.

explanation_wrong.already: Possible with surprise, but basic = yet.
Уже здесь, but question would be Are you already here?

title_ru: Give me that book / Give it to me
explanation_ru: Некоторые глаголы имеют два объекта: give, send, show. Можно сказать give me the book или give the book to me. Но с местоимением it/them обычно используем give it to me.
formula:
give + person + thing
give + thing + to + person
with pronoun thing: give it/them to me

common_mistakes.correct: Give me the book / Give the book to me.

explanation_wrong.for: Not with give in this meaning.
Wrong.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.to me: Can you lend your pen to me possible, but not before thing.

questions.question: Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: With it use give it to her.

explanation_ru: At, on, in используются со временем. At — точное время, on — дни и даты, in — месяцы, годы, времена года и части дня.
formula:
at + clock time
on + day/date
in + month/year/season/morning

explanation_wrong.on: On with days/dates.
explanation_wrong.in: In with months/years/parts of day.

explanation_wrong.at: At with exact time.
explanation_wrong.in: In with month/year.

explanation_wrong.on: On with dates/days.
explanation_wrong.at: At with exact time.

explanation_wrong.in: Month/year, not full date.
explanation_wrong.at: Exact clock time.

explanation_wrong.on: Date/day.
explanation_wrong.at: Exact time.

explanation_wrong.on: Not with night in this phrase.
explanation_wrong.in: In the morning/evening, but at night.

questions.question: Choose correct.
questions.explanation_correct: Part of day = in the evening.

Wrong unless specific evening: on Monday evening.

explanation_wrong.at: Exact time.
explanation_wrong.in: Months/years.

explanation_ru: From...to показывает начало и конец. Until = до момента. Since = с момента начала до сейчас. For = в течение периода.
formula:
from A to B
until + end point
since + start point
for + period

explanation_wrong.for: For + duration.
For + period.

questions.question: The shop is open ___ Monday to Friday.

explanation_wrong.since: Since Monday = from Monday until now.
explanation_wrong.for: For Monday wrong.

Would mean waited for midnight to happen; possible but not duration endpoint.

questions.question: She has worked here ___ last summer.

explanation_wrong.for: Last summer is point.
explanation_wrong.from: Would need from last summer to...

explanation_ru: Before и after могут быть предлогами или союзами. During — перед существительным: during the film. While — перед предложением с подлежащим и глаголом: while I was watching.
formula:
before/after + noun or clause
during + noun
while + subject + verb

examples.en: I fell asleep during the film.
I fell asleep while I was watching TV.

explanation_wrong.while: While + subject + verb.

questions.question: I slept ___ I was watching TV.

explanation_wrong.during: During lunch = во время lunch, possible but different.
explanation_wrong.while: Needs clause.
While eating possible, different meaning.
While I was on holiday, not while my holiday.

questions.question: He read a book ___ he waited.
Choose correct.
options: During the lesson, I took notes.
While the lesson, I took notes.
During I was studying, I took notes.
questions.correct_answer: During the lesson, I took notes.

explanation_ru: In — внутри пространства, on — на поверхности, at — в точке/месте события. Иногда выбор зависит от того, как мы воспринимаем место.
formula:
in + enclosed space/city/country
on + surface
at + point/place/event

explanation_wrong.in: Inside table? no.
explanation_wrong.at: Not for surface.

explanation_wrong.on: Not surface here.
explanation_wrong.at: Possible at home/work, but room uses in.

explanation_wrong.in: Inside station possible, but meeting point usually at.
explanation_wrong.on: Wrong.

questions.question: There is a picture ___ the wall.

explanation_wrong.in: Not inside wall.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.
At address/small point, not city.
explanation_wrong.on: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.in: In work not in this meaning.
explanation_wrong.on: Wrong.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: The cat is in the box.
The cat is on the box.
The cat is at the box.
questions.correct_answer: The cat is in the box.

questions.question: Write your name ___ the top of the page.

explanation_wrong.in: Not phrase.
explanation_wrong.on: On top of = physically above, not this phrase.

explanation_ru: Некоторые выражения с местом надо запоминать: in bed, in hospital, at home, at school, on a bus/train/plane, in a car/taxi.
formula:
in bed/hospital/prison
at home/work/school/university
on a bus/train/plane
in a car/taxi

explanation_wrong.in: In home wrong.
explanation_wrong.on: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.
explanation_wrong.on: Could be physically on top, but phrase is in bed.
On the train possible, but travelled by train is common.
explanation_wrong.in: Wrong.
Usually on bus.
explanation_wrong.at: At bus stop, not bus.

explanation_wrong.on: Not for taxi.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.in: Possible in American English sometimes, but unit basic = at.
explanation_wrong.on: Wrong.

questions.question: She works ___ a hospital.
questions.explanation_correct: Works in a hospital as building/institution; at also possible, but in is safe here.

explanation_wrong.on: Wrong.
explanation_wrong.at: Possible, but this example focuses in a hospital.

title_ru: To / in / at: movement and position
explanation_ru: To показывает движение к месту. In/at показывают нахождение. Не используем to после arrive: arrive in/at. Home часто без to: go home.
formula:
go/come/travel to
be/stay/live in/at
arrive in city/country
arrive at place/building

common_mistakes.explanation: After arrive use in/at, not to.

explanation_wrong.in: Position, not movement.
explanation_wrong.at: Point, not movement.
Not for city.

explanation_wrong.in: City/country use in.

explanation_wrong.at: At point/building.
At home = position.

explanation_wrong.in: Position.
explanation_wrong.at: Be at the party, not go at.

Can mean inside, but arrive at hotel is standard.

explanation_wrong.in: Not basic phrase.

explanation_ru: Эти предлоги показывают положение предметов: under — под, behind — за, in front of — перед, next to — рядом, between — между, opposite — напротив.
formula: under / behind / in front of / next to / between / opposite

examples.en: The cat is under the table.
The bank is opposite the station.
She is sitting between Tom and Anna.
examples.ru: Она сидит между Томом и Анной.

common_mistakes.explanation: In basic British usage no to.

questions.question: There is a tree ___ the house.
Choose correct.
options: The shop is opposite the bank.
The shop is opposite to the bank.
The shop is under the bank.
questions.correct_answer: The shop is opposite the bank.

explanation_ru: Эти предлоги показывают движение: up/down — вверх/вниз, into/out of — внутрь/наружу, through — через внутри пространства, across — через поверхность/пространство, over — над/через сверху.
formula: into / out of / up / down / through / across / over / along / past

examples.en: The bird flew over the house.

common_mistakes.explanation: Movement from outside to inside = into.

explanation_wrong.in: Position, not movement.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.out: Need out of + noun.

explanation_wrong.up: Вверх.

questions.question: Walk ___ this street until the end.
Choose correct.
options: She ran out of the room.
questions.correct_answer: She ran out of the room.

explanation_wrong.through: Through would be through an opening.

explanation_ru: Многие предлоги используются в устойчивых выражениях: by car, on foot, with someone, about something, at a party, on holiday. Их нужно запоминать блоками.
formula:
by + transport
on foot
with + person/tool
about + topic
at + event/place

examples.en: I go to work by car.
examples.ru: Я езжу на работу на машине.

explanation_wrong.on: On the bus possible, but came by bus is method.

explanation_wrong.by: By for agent/method, but tool usually with.
explanation_wrong.on: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.by: By my friend = сделано другом, wrong.

explanation_wrong.on: Can be formal topic, but basic = about.
On a party wrong.

Need a/my credit card, but basic phrase by credit card.

explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.
explanation_wrong.in: Wrong.

explanation_ru: После многих прилагательных нужен конкретный предлог: afraid of, good at, interested in, fed up with. Если после предлога идёт глагол, он принимает -ing.
formula:
adjective + preposition + noun/V-ing
good at doing
afraid of doing
interested in doing

explanation_wrong.in: Not in this phrase.

explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.
explanation_wrong.from: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.
Wrong.
explanation_wrong.in: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

Possible in some contexts, but basic structure here is interested in -ing.

explanation_wrong.to: Afraid to do possible, but with flying as activity use of.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

title_ru: Listen to / look at: verb + preposition
explanation_ru: Некоторые глаголы требуют определённый предлог: listen to, look at, wait for, belong to, depend on. В русском предлог может быть другим или отсутствовать.
formula:
listen to
look at
wait for
belong to
depend on
ask for

explanation_wrong.at: Look at, not listen at.
explanation_wrong.for: Wait for.
Look for = search.

explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.for: Wrong.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.from: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.to: Ask someone to do, but ask for noun.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

Look at = смотреть на, not искать.

explanation_wrong.at: Talk at = говорить в сторону, not normal conversation.
explanation_wrong.for: Wrong.

title_ru: Phrasal verbs 1: go in / fall off / run away
explanation_ru: Фразовые глаголы состоят из глагола и частицы: go in, come back, sit down, run away. Частица часто сильно меняет значение.
formula:
verb + particle
come back, sit down, get up, run away, fall off

explanation_wrong.on: Come on другой смысл.

explanation_wrong.in: Fall in = упасть внутрь.

explanation_wrong.off: Run off possible, but away basic.
explanation_wrong.in: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.out: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.away: Go away = уйти прочь, but not of the room.

title_ru: Phrasal verbs 2: put on your shoes / put your shoes on
explanation_ru: У некоторых фразовых глаголов объект может стоять между глаголом и частицей или после частицы: put on your shoes / put your shoes on. Но местоимение почти всегда ставим между: put them on.
formula:
verb + particle + noun
verb + noun + particle
verb + pronoun + particle

common_mistakes.explanation: Pronoun goes between verb and particle.

common_mistakes.wrong: Take off your shoes / Take your shoes off
common_mistakes.explanation: Noun can be in two positions.

explanation_wrong.off: Would mean снять? take off, not put off shoes.

explanation_wrong.on: Wrong.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.on: Wrong.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

explanation_wrong.in: Wrong.
explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.

explanation_ru: Эти союзы соединяют идеи. And добавляет, but показывает контраст, or выбор, so результат, because причину.
formula:
and = addition
but = contrast
or = choice
so = result
because = reason

examples.en: I was tired, so I went home.
I went home because I was tired.
examples.ru: Я пошёл домой, потому что устал.

examples.en: She is nice but very quiet.

common_mistakes.wrong: I was tired because I went home.
common_mistakes.correct: I went home because I was tired.

common_mistakes.wrong: I was tired, because I went home.
common_mistakes.correct: I was tired, so I went home.

explanation_wrong.because: Would introduce reason, not result here.

questions.question: It was late, ___ we took a taxi.

explanation_wrong.because: Reason would need because it was late.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: I stayed home because it was raining.
I stayed home so it was raining.
I stayed home but it was raining.
questions.correct_answer: I stayed home because it was raining.

explanation_wrong.and: Could be, but or gives warning.

explanation_ru: When может вводить время: When I arrived, she was cooking. В придаточном времени о будущем обычно используем Present Simple, не will: When I see him, I will tell him.
formula:
When + clause
Future meaning: When + Present Simple, ... will ...

examples.en: When I arrived, she was cooking.
When I see him, I will tell him.
examples.ru: Когда я его увижу, я скажу ему.

examples.en: Call me when you get home.

common_mistakes.explanation: After when for future use Present Simple.

common_mistakes.wrong: Call me when you will arrive

common_mistakes.correct: When I arrived, she was cooking

questions.question: When I ___ him, I will tell him.
questions.explanation_correct: Future time clause uses Present Simple.

explanation_wrong.will see: No will after when in time clause.

questions.question: I was sleeping when the phone ___.

explanation_wrong.was ringing: Could describe background, but phone started ringing = rang.

questions.question: When she ___ tomorrow, we will start.
questions.explanation_correct: After when use present for future.

questions.question: I'll wait here until you ___.
questions.explanation_correct: Until like when: present for future.

questions.question: Choose correct.
options: When I finish, I'll call you.
When I will finish, I'll call you.
When I finished, I'll call you.
questions.correct_answer: When I finish, I'll call you.
questions.explanation_correct: Future time clause uses Present Simple.

questions.question: What will you do when you ___ school?

explanation_ru: First conditional говорит о реальном возможном будущем: if + Present Simple, will + verb. В части с if обычно не используем will.
formula:
if + Present Simple, will + verb
will + verb if + Present Simple

examples.en: If it rains, I will stay home.
examples.ru: Если пойдёт дождь, я останусь дома.

examples.en: If I see him, I'll tell him.
examples.ru: Если я его увижу, я ему скажу.

examples.en: I'll call you if I need help.

common_mistakes.explanation: No will in if-clause for real future.

common_mistakes.wrong: If I see him, I tell him
common_mistakes.correct: If I see him, I will tell him

common_mistakes.wrong: I will call if I will need
common_mistakes.correct: I will call if I need

questions.question: If I see her, I ___ tell her.
I'll help you if I ___ time.
questions.explanation_correct: After if use present for future.

questions.question: What will you do if you ___ the exam?
If he ___ late, we won't wait.
She will be happy if you ___.
Choose correct.
options: If I have money, I'll buy it.
If I will have money, I'll buy it.
If I had money, I'll buy it.
questions.correct_answer: If I have money, I'll buy it.

questions.question: If you don't hurry, you ___ miss the train.

explanation_ru: Second conditional говорит о нереальной или маловероятной ситуации сейчас/в будущем: If I had more money, I would travel. После if используем Past Simple, в результате would + verb.
formula:
if + Past Simple, would + verb
would + verb if + Past Simple

examples.en: If I had more money, I would travel.
examples.ru: Если бы у меня было больше денег, я бы путешествовал.

examples.en: If I were you, I would wait.
examples.ru: На твоём месте я бы подождал.

examples.en: She would buy it if it was cheaper.
examples.ru: Она бы купила это, если бы оно было дешевле.

common_mistakes.wrong: If I will have money, I would travel.
common_mistakes.correct: If I had money, I would travel.
common_mistakes.explanation: Second conditional uses past after if.

common_mistakes.wrong: If I had money, I will travel.
common_mistakes.correct: If I had money, I would travel.

common_mistakes.explanation: Fixed/formal phrase: If I were you.

questions.question: If I ___ rich, I would travel.
questions.explanation_correct: Unreal condition uses past; were is standard.

explanation_wrong.am: Present form not for second conditional.

questions.question: If she had time, she ___ help us.
I would buy it if it ___ cheaper.

explanation_wrong.is: Present form gives first conditional-ish meaning.

questions.question: If I ___ you, I wouldn't go.
questions.explanation_correct: Fixed phrase: If I were you.

questions.question: What would you do if you ___ your job?
If we lived near the sea, we ___ swim every day.
Choose correct.
options: If I knew, I would tell you.
If I know, I would tell you.
If I would know, I told you.
questions.correct_answer: If I knew, I would tell you.

questions.question: He would be happier if he ___ more free time.

title_ru: Relative clauses 1: a person who / a thing that
explanation_ru: Relative clauses добавляют информацию о человеке или вещи. Who — для людей, which/that — для вещей/животных. На русском часто переводится «который».
formula:
person + who/that + verb
thing + which/that + verb

examples.en: A teacher is a person who teaches.
This is the book that I bought.
I met a woman who lives next door.
examples.ru: Я встретил женщину, которая живёт рядом.

questions.question: A doctor is a person ___ helps sick people.

explanation_wrong.which: Which for things.

questions.question: This is the camera ___ I bought.
I know a woman ___ speaks five languages.

explanation_wrong.which: Which for things.

questions.question: A fridge is a thing ___ keeps food cold.
The man ___ lives upstairs is noisy.

explanation_wrong.which: Things.

questions.question: The film ___ we saw was good.
Choose correct.
options: I need a phone that takes good photos.
I need a phone who takes good photos.
I need a phone what takes good photos.
questions.correct_answer: I need a phone that takes good photos.

questions.question: A waiter is someone ___ serves food.

explanation_wrong.which: Things.

title_ru: Relative clauses 2: the people we met
explanation_ru: В object relative clauses who/that/which часто можно опустить: The people we met. The hotel we stayed at. Если есть предлог, он часто стоит в конце.
formula:
object relative: noun + subject + verb
The man I saw
The hotel we stayed at

examples.en: The people we met were nice.
examples.ru: Люди, которых мы встретили, были милыми.

examples.en: The hotel we stayed at was cheap.
examples.ru: Отель, в котором мы остановились, был дешёвым.

examples.en: The book I told you about is good.
examples.ru: Книга, о которой я тебе говорил, хорошая.

common_mistakes.wrong: The hotel where we stayed at
common_mistakes.correct: The hotel we stayed at / the hotel where we stayed
common_mistakes.explanation: Не используем where и at вместе.

common_mistakes.wrong: The book about I told you
common_mistakes.correct: The book I told you about

questions.question: The people ___ we met were friendly.
questions.explanation_correct: Object relative pronoun can be omitted.

explanation_wrong.them: Object already included in relative clause.

questions.question: The book I told you ___ is on the table.

explanation_wrong.for: Wrong preposition.

questions.question: The hotel we stayed ___ was expensive.

explanation_wrong.in: Stay in possible, but unit phrase uses at.

questions.question: The woman I spoke ___ was kind.

explanation_wrong.at: Speak at different meaning.
explanation_wrong.for: Wrong.

questions.question: The film we watched ___ boring.
questions.explanation_correct: The film was; no extra it.

questions.question: The man ___ I saw was tall.

options: The restaurant we went to was full.
The restaurant where we went to was full.
The restaurant we went was full.
questions.correct_answer: The restaurant we went to was full.
questions.explanation_correct: Go to a restaurant; preposition at end.

questions.question: The music we listened ___ was beautiful.

explanation_wrong.at: Wrong.
explanation_wrong.for: Wrong.

explanation_ru: Используй Present Continuous, когда действие происходит сейчас, длится вокруг настоящего, является временной ситуацией или изменением. Это не только «в эту секунду», а ещё «в этот период»: I’m learning English this year. Your English is getting better.
formula: am/is/are + V-ing

title_ru: Present Continuous vs Present Simple: временное или постоянное
explanation_ru: Continuous показывает временную ситуацию или процесс сейчас. Simple показывает постоянную ситуацию, факт или привычку. I’m living with friends now, but my parents live in London.
formula: temporary: am/is/are + V-ing; permanent/general: Present Simple
